summary_markdown: The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines five primary business processes that span initial registration through ongoing care management. Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to capture, maintain, and utilize person-centered information effectively.

activities.objectives: Establish person's identity, capture core demographics, and register individual requirements including reasonable adjustments
activities.personas: GP/Primary Care Physician, Care Home Manager, Social Worker
activities.title: Initial Person Registration & Demographics Capture

activities.objectives: Conduct comprehensive health assessment, identify care needs, and develop person-centered care plan
activities.personas: Hospital Consultant, Community Nurse, GP/Primary Care Physician, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: Clinical Assessment & Care Planning

activities.objectives: Rapid access to critical information during emergency situations to ensure appropriate and safe care delivery
activities.personas: Paramedic/Emergency Responder, Hospital Consultant, Family Carer
activities.title: Emergency Response & Information Access

activities.objectives: Assess social care needs, determine support requirements, and coordinate care package delivery
activities.personas: Social Worker, Care Home Manager, Community Nurse, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: Social Care Needs Assessment

activities.objectives: Establish person's end of life preferences, document advance decisions, and coordinate palliative care
activities.personas: GP/Primary Care Physician, Community Nurse, Hospital Consultant, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: End of Life Care Planning
